about: The consumer's demand ledger — phrases the app needs that the GOLD catalog (assets.db) does not have yet. This is the mirror of the producer's assets.db: they publish supply, we publish demand, each side owns exactly one source file. See docs/audio-clip-contract.md §8.
invariants: This file lists ONLY open requests. There is no status field: presence here == open.

Fulfillment is migration, not a flag. When the producer promotes a phrase, its asset_id moves into clips/clips.manifest.json and the request is DELETED from here. 'Done' == 'gone', so a request can never be marked-done-yet-still-listed (the drift trap the assets.json removal designed out).
In-use dependencies are NOT duplicated here. clips/clips.manifest.json is the authoritative list of asset_ids we depend on; read that for 'don't de-curate these'.
Run scripts/export_requests.py to reconcile this ledger against current gold (OPEN / READY / STALE).

used_in: causality/7.1 — spider climbs UP the spout
requests.note: Completes the in/out/up/down concept vocabulary. 'out' is already gold (asset_d703e812fbff) in your new Words group; up/down/in are the natural siblings the causality and spatial scenes want.

used_in: causality/7.1 — water washes the spider DOWN
spatial/4.1 — put the ball IN the box
peekaboo — the animal is hiding IN
